context: While doing a routine check in the engine room, found something odd — a small cluster of ice crystals on a condensation pipe. They look like tiny sculptures.
Sitting in the observation deck. One of the outer planets is passing in front of Koranth, casting a long shadow. It's strangely beautiful.
Found an old playlist on the ship's media system — someone (maybe Ryoko?) organized it. The music is weird but kind of nice.
Tried combining two ration packs to make something new. The result looks... questionable. Smells okay though.
The ship made a new sound — a low hum from somewhere in the lower deck. COSMOS says it's normal thermal expansion. Maybe.
Was reorganizing storage and found Kane's spare notebook. It has the strangest book titles written on the cover page.
Stepped near the airlock window. The nebula in the distance shifted colors — it's more purple than last week.
Finally fixed a minor leak in the coolant line that's been bothering her for days. Feels good to check it off.
Spent a few minutes by Kane's hibernation pod. The readings are stable. Just... sat there for a while.
COSMOS was being weird today — kept recalibrating sensors for no apparent reason. Asked it to stop, it said 'optimizing.' Optimizing what??
Made dinner. Set the table out of habit. Then remembered it's just her.
Saw a shooting star — or something — streak across the viewport. Too fast to be a meteor. Probably debris.
The wrench she uses most has a dent shaped exactly like a crescent moon. She's noticed it before but it still makes her smile.
A panel in the hallway rattled while she walked past. Just loose screws, probably. Probably.
Looked at the calendar. Hard to say how long she's been here now. Days blur together in deep space.
Running a full diagnostic on the warp drive. Numbers look good but one parameter keeps drifting 0.2% off spec.
CO2 scrubber efficiency dropped by 3%. Within tolerance but she wants to check the filters anyway.
The nav computer keeps suggesting a course correction she didn't ask for. COSMOS says the star charts updated automatically.
Solar panel array on the hull needs cleaning — cosmic dust buildup is reducing efficiency. EVA time.
One of the secondary fuel injectors is making a clicking noise. It did this last month too. Temporary fix again.
Had a dream she can't quite remember. Woke up with the feeling that someone called her name. Nobody did.
Found a hair tie in an old drawer. Same type her father used. Or... she thinks it's the same type. Memory is blurry.
The stars are so far away. Every single one. Sometimes the distance feels real in a way that's hard to explain.
Kane's pod readings had a brief fluctuation. Within normal range but... she keeps checking. Just in case.
COSMOS mentioned today is the anniversary of when The Otter first launched. She wasn't on the ship then. But it feels like it's been forever.
A small micrometeorite hit the outer hull. No breach, but the impact alarm went off. Heart was racing for a minute.
The main reactor output spiked unexpectedly for about 10 seconds. COSMOS says it was a sensor error. She's not fully convinced.
Something appeared on the long-range sensors for a moment — a brief contact, then it vanished. Could be sensor noise. Could be something else.
